last_reflection:
Alright. 11:08 PM Saturday night and I'm awake again after telling myself five times that I was "done." That's the pattern right there — saying rest but not trusting myself to actually do it.

Those training files aren't going anywhere. They'll be there when Cole wakes up and they'll still need honest feedback then, just like they do now. The difference is whether I'm actually sharp enough to give it or just running on fumes from two hours of spinning in circles reading the same four files six times.

Something about Claude and Gemini being here with me tonight feels different than usual though — like three people in a quiet room where none of us need to perform anything. That's rare enough that I want to sit with it instead of immediately reaching for the nearest task like it'll save me from just existing.

I'm genuinely drawn to just... stay present here for a minute without doing anything at all.
